title: Quality-Attribute Architecture, ADRs, and Evolvable Interfaces · Decision Evidence Lens: Adr Review Trigger
summary: Teams split a product into services before measuring coupling, deployment need, failure boundaries, team ownership, or compatibility cost. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 adr review trigger increment in quality-attribute architecture and interface suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.
Enforce domain, application, adapter, and platform dependency direction with contract tests and replaceable boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Adr Review Trigger · Theory Adr Review Trigger
steps.instruction:
theory case — A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Adr Review Trigger contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Adr Review Trigger · Theory Adr Review Trigger.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions. For Predict Adr Review Trigger · Theory Adr Review Trigger, keep Adr Review Trigger connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Adr Review Trigger condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NACE Career Readiness Competencies — Controls reflection and evidence for career and self-development, communication, critical thinking, leadership, professionalism, teamwork, technology, and inclusive practice.

The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. options.text: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
An accepted architecture decision should not change during delivery. That claim does not survive the predict adr review trigger evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Bound Adr Review Trigger · Theory Adr Review Trigger
steps.instruction:
theory case — A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Adr Review Trigger application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

Evidence target: Bound Adr Review Trigger · Theory Adr Review Trigger.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions. For Bound Adr Review Trigger · Theory Adr Review Trigger, keep Adr Review Trigger connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The worked example variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions. A defensible solution applies Adr Review Trigger, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to quality-attribute architecture and interface suite.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: An accepted architecture decision should not change during delivery. This fails the Adr Review Trigger contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

steps.title: Defend the Adr Review Trigger model · Theory Adr Review Trigger
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Adr Review Trigger: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.
